name: Salmon for Louise
recipeIngredient: 12 oz Fillets of salmon dusted with flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Dust 2 salmon fillets (6 oz each) with flour. Shake off excess.
2. In a sauté pan, heat clarified butter over medium heat.
3. Add fillets and brown on one side; turn.
4. Add garlic, shallots, red peppers, and chopped cilantro to the pan.
5. Deglaze the pan with dry sherry.
6. Add fish stock and reduce for 2-3 minutes.
7. Stir in cream and reduce until sauce thickens.
8. Remove salmon fillets to a heated serving plate.
9. In the same pan, add 2-3 tablespoons Madeira and cook for an additional minute to evaporate alcohol.
10. Spoon sauce over the salmon, garnish with crème fraiche, and serve with cilantro sprig.
NOTES:
When I made this dish, I didn't have fresh chanterelles, so I used dried Shitake mushrooms soaked in hot water for about 10 minutes before rinsing and slicing them. I also skipped the cilantro because my husband dislikes it. The key is not to overcook the salmon; remove it from the pan after deglazing to prevent an unpleasant dry texture. I only used tail fillets to avoid bones. I used roasted red peppers in place of Christy red peppers, made fish stock with the fish's head, bones, and trimmings, and strained it through cheesecloth to remove any cloudy pieces.
